titleStmt.title: Introduction to the Site of Mount Dazhai

change.item: Abschnitt Carving Techniques überarbeitet gemäß Markette
neues Inhaltsverzeichnis angelegt und Text eingefüllt
Abschnitt Related Monasteries geändert auf Verweis auf Mount Tianchi.
Änderungen und Kürzungen von LL eingearbeitet

figure.head: Documentation of the Buddha name carved on Mount Dazhai; photograph taken in 2006
記錄大寨山刊刻的佛名，2006年照片

div.head: 地理位置
div.p: 大寨山位於魯中山脈呈南北方向的大寨谷之內，崖壁極其陡峭（）。它位於平陰縣東南偏東25公里、聊城市東南51公里、東平縣西北20公里處（見封二地圖）。 山腳海拔高度約為140米。目前摩崖上没有現代人工開鑿痕跡，附近也沒有任何建築。

大寨山的地理位置是東經116.3235度，北緯36.0791度，相當於UTM坐標系統第50S帶，東行439090，北行3992930。摩崖石刻面向北方， 在大寨山接近峰頂的崖壁之上。大寨山西北3.5公里，有天池山和書院東山石刻（地圖，），但被大寨山的其他幾條山脊所遮擋而在此處遠眺視線所及之外。附近的雲翠山石刻，在其西南偏西方向3.3公里處。在大寨山上，可以看到雲翠山，但其上摩崖石刻卻在大寨山遠眺視線所及之外，因石刻在天柱峰正下方面西。

div.head: 地形與空間構成
div.p: 大寨山僅有一處題刻，刻在靠近山巔一條朝北的山脊上的一塊平整大岩石上面（）。

div.head: 釋名
div.p: 山上有明代以來當地民眾為躲避戰亂而修建的石房數十間，相連成寨，故山有“大寨山”之名。

div.head: 相關寺院
div.p: 參照天池山相關部分。

div.head: 相關人物
div.p: 無此處摩崖石刻及其附近寺院人物的歷史紀錄。根據其書法風格判斷，此銘刻應亦為6世紀下半葉山東境內刻經僧團所作。

div.head: 斷代
div.p: 大寨山題刻無可供斷代的相關信息，不過其書法風格及刊刻技法，說明它可能出自北齊時期。

div.head: 研究史
div.p: 大寨山摩崖石刻的相關研究不多，見於山東省石刻藝術博物館編輯的《北朝摩崖刻經研究［續］》。在此論文集中，此處題刻數次被提及。張總4；賴非104, 120；田熊信之180；桐谷征一51, 74。

div.head: 銘文總數
div.p: 大寨山刊刻有一個佛名（大寨山1號）。

div.head: 大寨山1號
div.p: 佛名“阿弥阤佛”縱向刊刻在水平崖面之上。該佛名（又“阿彌陀佛”），在崗山刊刻了兩次（崗山1.1號和3號），洪頂山一次（洪頂山9.10號）， 徂徠山一次，還有一次在陶山。但僅有大寨山的佛名使用了雙鈎的刻法。第三個字“阤”，是“陀”字的異構字。崗山石刻也使用了這同一異構字，而洪頂山、徂徠山和陶山使用了 “陀”字的另一個異構字──“陁”。不過，就書法風格來看，大寨山的佛名與洪頂山和陶山的寫法相近。

div.head: 刊刻技法
div.p: 從筆道横斷面上對刻經技法進行分析，我們認為大寨山刻經技法主要為“凵” 形刻法及“雙鈎”刻法。

figure.head: North-facing ridge of Mount Dazhai; photograph taken in 2006
大寨山北麓，2006年照片

div.head: Location
div.p: Mount Dazhai 大寨山 lies within the north-south oriented Dazhai Valley 大寨谷 of Shandong’s central ranges. Its slopes rise steeply (fig. ). It is situated 25 km east-southeast of the county seat of Pingyin 平陰, 51 km southeast of Liaocheng City 聊城市, and 20 km northwest of the county seat of Dongping 東平 (map in front endpaper). The foot of Mount Dazhai is at about 140 m above sea level. So far, it has been unaffected by human activity, and no construction has occurred nearby.

Geographically, Mount Dazhai is situated 116.3235° east and 36.0791° north WGS84. This equals 439090 E (easting) and 3992930 N (northing) on the meridian 50S UTM. The inscription can be found on a ridge close to the summit of Mount Dazhai, facing north. The inscriptions of Mount Tianchi 天池山 and East Mount Shuyuan 書院東山 are 3.5 km to the northwest of Mount Dazhai (map, fig. ). They are blocked from view by several other ridges of Mount Dazhai. The inscriptions on neighboring Mount Yuncui 雲翠山 are situated 3.3 km to the west-southwest. Mount Yuncui can be seen from Mount Dazhai, but not the inscriptions, because they were carved right below Heavenly Column Peak 天柱峰 and are facing west.

div.head: Topography and Spatial Grouping
div.p: Mount Dazhai has only one single inscription; it is located on a ridge facing north close to the summit, on the flat surface of a horizontal boulder (fig. ).

div.head: Place Name
div.p: From Ming dynasty times, several dozen houses were built on the mountain. Here, locals hid themselves to escape the chaos of various wars. These houses form a camp; therefore the mountain was named “Mount Great Camp”, Dazhai Shan 大寨山.

div.head: Related Monasteries
div.p: See above the relevant introduction to Mount Tianchi 天池山.

div.head: Related Persons
div.p: No historical record available. Calligraphy produced by members of the same group, who worked elsewhere in Shandong.

div.head: Dating
div.p: No information exists on the dating of Mount Dazhai’s inscription, apart from the fact that the calligraphic style and carving technique point to the Northern Qi dynasty.

div.head: Research History
div.p: The limited research on this site is found in Research on the Sutras Engraved on Polished Cliffs of the Northern Dynasties, vol. 2 (Beichao moya kejing yanjiu (xu) 北朝摩崖刻經研究（續）). 4; 104 and 120; 180; 51 and 74.

div.head: Inventory
div.p: One Buddha name (DZ 1) was carved.

div.head: DZ 1
div.p: The name “Buddha Amitābha” was carved in one vertical column. This name was also carved once at Mount Hongding 洪頂山 (HDS 9.10), twice at Mount Gang 崗山 (GS D1 and GS D3), once at Mount Tao 陶山, and once at Mount Culai 徂徠山. The name at Mount Dazhai is the only example executed in double outlines. The third character, reading tuo 阤, is a variant of the character 陀. Only the inscriptions at Mount Gang use the same variant; those at Mount Hongding, Mount Culai, and Mount Tao use the character 陁, another variant of 陀. Nevertheless, in calligraphic style, the Buddha name of Mount Da­zhai is closest to the inscriptions at Mount Hongding and Mount Tao.

div.head: Carving Techniques
div.p: The name is in rectangular U-shape carving and double-outline carving.
